title: Do not default to three-column pricing tables
summary: AI consistently generates a three-column pricing table with a highlighted 'Popular' middle tier, checkmark features, and annual/monthly toggle, regardless of whether the product has three pricing tiers.
instruction: Choose a pricing presentation that matches the actual pricing model. Consider single-column feature matrices, usage-based scales, custom enterprise pricing, or sliding cost calculators before defaulting to three columns.
rationale: The three-column table with 'Popular' badge is a template pattern, not a design decision. It imposes a three-tier structure that may not fit the product and trains users to ignore the 'Popular' signal.
violations: Three identical columns with the middle one highlighted as 'Popular'
Feature checkmarks in the same monotone color on every row
Annual/monthly toggle above the table as a pill switch
'Get Started' CTAs in every column with identical styling
Pricing that could describe any SaaS product with no product-specific framing
controls: Pricing shown as a single-column feature matrix with a call to contact sales for custom pricing
Usage-based calculator that shows price based on user inputs
Two-tier structure when the product genuinely has a free and a pro tier
Custom enterprise section distinct from standard pricing
borderlineCases: Actual three-tier products where the middle tier genuinely serves most customers
Developer tools offering free/team/enterprise as a genuine industry convention
contextualExceptions: Template-driven starter sites where pricing is not the primary differentiator
detectorSignal: Three columnar containers each containing a price, a heading, and a feature list within close proximity on the page, combined with a highlighted middle column.
matcherProposal: Identify pricing-section landmarks or columns containing currency symbols, feature checkmarks (✓ or check-circle SVG), and at least three side-by-side card elements with a distinguished middle card.
detectorLimitations: Cannot distinguish genuine three-tier pricing from the template pattern
Custom pricing pages that genuinely need three columns
falsePositiveScenarios: Products with actual three pricing tiers that fit the column layout
Enterprise SaaS landing pages following industry convention
mitigationStrategy: Flag as warning with context about pricing model fit rather than as error.

fixturePlans.violation: Three-column pricing with 'Popular' middle badge, green checkmarks, monthly/annual toggle, and identical CTAs.
fixturePlans.control: Single-column feature matrix with two pricing options side-by-side in a non-column layout.
fixturePlans.borderline: Three-column pricing for a product that genuinely has three distinct plans with different target users.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Not covered by existing rules. Related to layout.centered-everything but addresses the specific pricing table template.
